free_text: Alpha-gamma angular correlations in the inelastic
scattering of alpha particles on 24Mg at E(a)=30.3 MeV.
A detailed description of experimental setup and of the
enriched to 97% magnesium film 0.77 mg/cm**2 thick
with a sensitive region up to 400 mum thick were used
to detect the final alpha particles.
Gamma rays were detected by four scintillation
detectors BDEG-23 based on NaI(Tl) crystals 63 mm in
The error in the absolute values of the differential
cross section was on average about 20% and was
determined primarily by the uncertainties in measuring
the target thickness and in calibrating the current
.The target thickness error did not exceed
differential cross sections for each angle
Ang1 were found from the condition A00(ang1)=dsigma/
domega(ang1). In this case, there were no problems in
monitoring relative efficiency or the absolute
calibration of the charged particle detectors.
